settings.title: Settings
settings.updater_action_check: Mark only — no notification, no download
settings.data_wizard_step_mode: Step 2: Choose how to handle data
settings.data_wizard_pick_desc: The data directory is where SG Hub keeps all your papers, Skills, chat history and PDFs. Pick somewhere with plenty of space and that's easy to back up.
settings.data_wizard_existing_found: This path already has SG Hub data (consider "Use existing data")
settings.data_wizard_mode_migrate: Migrate existing data to the new location (recommended)
settings.data_wizard_mode_migrate_desc: Copy everything to the new path and switch. Best when moving to a new machine or cleaning up your folders.
settings.data_wizard_mode_fresh: Start fresh at the new location
settings.data_wizard_mode_fresh_desc: Don't migrate anything; the new directory starts empty. For advanced users running multiple isolated environments.
settings.data_wizard_mode_use_existing: Use the SG Hub data already at the new location
settings.data_wizard_mode_use_existing_desc: Switch to that path and reuse the existing data. Useful for restoring backups or sharing across devices (OneDrive / iCloud Drive).
settings.data_wizard_warn: Don't close the app while this runs. SG Hub needs a restart to use the new location.
settings.data_wizard_restart_prompt: Restart SG Hub to load the new data directory.
settings.data_section_desc: Where SG Hub stores the database, PDFs, and cache.
settings.data_reset_default_done: Restored to the default data directory
settings.updater_section_desc: When enabled, SG Hub checks GitHub Releases on the schedule below.
settings.privacy_section_desc: How SG Hub handles your data — local storage, API keys, model calls, literature search, and every other data flow.
settings.privacy_subtitle: You can switch the reading language independently — this does not change your app language.

store.title: AI Store
store.subtitle: Prepaid model packs — no API key application required. Provided by SG AI Store; SG Hub is just the client.
store.byok_notice: BYOK users carry on — AI Store is optional. You can still configure your own keys for Anthropic / OpenAI / DeepSeek / Ollama in Models.
store.privacy_disclosure: AI Store models route through the sgaistore.com gateway. The gateway logs usage metadata only — paper content is not stored. See
store.faq_q_byok: Can I still use my own API key alongside this?
store.faq_a_byok: Yes. AI Store packs and the API keys you configure yourself in Models are independent. AI Parse / Chat route by ModelConfig, regardless of how the model was paid for.
store.faq_q_quota: How is the token quota counted?
store.faq_a_quota: By the token count reported by the SG AI Store gateway (input + output combined), accumulated across the billing period and reset at the start of the next one. Remaining balance is visible per-card under Models (lands in Session 29).
store.faq_q_refund: Can I cancel and get a refund?
store.faq_a_refund: Monthly packs are non-refundable. Yearly packs support prorated refunds based on days remaining. Final terms are governed by the purchase page on sgaistore.com.
store.terms_disclosure: Purchases are governed by the sgaistore.com Terms of Service. For the full data-flow breakdown see
store.confirm_buy_desc: Your browser will open the sgaistore.com checkout for {{product}} (¥{{price}}). After purchase, come back to SG Hub → Models and paste the API key SG AI Store gives you to start using it.
store.toast_browser_opened_desc: After purchase, return to Models to paste your API key.

balance_dialog.title: SG AI Store balance too low
balance_dialog.subtitle: Top up your account, or switch to another model for now.
balance_dialog.subtitle_with_model: Model "{{model}}" is out of credit. Top up, or switch to a different model.
balance_dialog.body: Your SG AI Store balance has dropped below the minimum needed to keep making calls (¥1 / 1000 tokens). "Top up" opens the sgaistore.com recharge page in your browser; you can also keep working by switching to a model you've configured with your own API key.
